explanation: This command uses the original syntax and flags from the Sigma rule. It checks permissions for 'Domain Users' on the System32 directory.
Single quotes are used instead of double quotes to wrap the group name, which is an equivalent syntax in many command-line environments.
The group name is rewritten using the domain\user format, which is a valid alternative representation in Windows command-line tools.
The slash is replaced with a backslash in the group name, which is a valid alternative representation in Windows command-line tools.
The command is reordered with no change in functionality, as the target directory and group name are still valid.
The command is encoded using environment variables to obscure the original command line, which may bypass detection.
